```
  ├── "citation_expander" ────────────────────────────────────────────┐                               │
  ├── "continue" ──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │                               │
  ├── "blocked" ──────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │  │                               │
  └── "END" ─────────────────── (terminal)                    │    │  │                               │
                                                               │    │  │                               │
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │  │                               │
    ▼                                                               │  │                               │
[final_recommendation]                                              │  │                               │
    │                                                               │  │                               │
    ▼                                                               │  │                               │
   END                                                              │  │                               │
                                                                    │  │                               │
    ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │                               │
    ▼                                                                  │                               │
[citation_expander]    reads: verified_papers, topic_atoms             │                               │
    │                   writes: seed_papers, expanded_papers,          │                               │
    │                           surveys_found, repos_found,            │                               │
    │                           citation_expansion_done, paper_candidates│                             │
    │                                                                  │                               │
    └──► [verify] ──► [quality_gate]  (second round with citation_expansion_done=True)              │
                                                                       │                               │
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘                               │
    ▼                                                                                                  │
[dataset_repo_extractor]  reads: verified_papers | writes: dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, evidence_audit
    │
    ▼
[evidence_graph_builder]  reads: verified_papers, dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, baseline_candidates, work_packages
    │                      writes: evidence_graph, evidence_audit
    ▼
[baseline_classifier]     reads: verified_papers | writes: baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates, dataset_papers, surveys, evidence_audit
    │
    ▼
[feasibility_assessor]    reads: baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates, dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, topic
    │                      writes: feasibility_report
    │
    ├── "work_package" ──────────────────────────────────────┐
    └── "optimization_advisor" ───────────────────────────┐  │
                                                          │  │
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
    ▼                                                        │
[work_package]  reads: topic, topic_atoms, baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates,
    │                   dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, user_constraints
    │            writes: work_packages, evidence_audit
    │
    ├──► [innovation_extractor]  reads: topic, baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates
    │          writes: innovation_points, stitching_plan
    │
    └──► [sota_matcher]  reads: topic, baseline_candidates
               writes: sota_comparison
    │
    ├── (fan-in) ────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    └── (fan-in) ────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
                                                        │   │
    ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
    ▼                                                       │
[narrative_builder]  reads: topic, innovation_points, feasibility_report
    │                 writes: research_narrative
    ▼
[low_bar_review]     reads: work_packages, verified_papers, baseline_candidates,
    │                        parallel_candidates, dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, evidence_graph
    │                 writes: low_bar_review, work_packages (filtered)
    │
    ├── "repair" ──► [targeted_repair] ──► [paper_retriever] (loops back to retrieval chain)
    ├── "ready" ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    └── "blocked" ──► [final_recommendation] ──► END                           │
                                                                               │
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    │                    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    ▼                    ▼
[optimization_advisor]  reads: topic, feasibility_report, innovation_points, baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates
    │                    writes: optimization_directions
    ▼
[devils_advocate]       reads: baseline_candidates, feasibility_report, (narrative context)
    │                    writes: review_report
    │
    ├── "human_gate" ───────────────────────────────────────────┐
    ├── "narrative_builder" ──► (loops back, incrementing narrative_revision_count, max 2)
    └── "optimization_advisor" ──► (loops back, incrementing devils_advocate_block_count, max 1)
    
    ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    ▼
[human_gate]            reads: verified_papers, work_packages | writes: human_gate
    │
    ▼
[final_recommendation]  reads: verified_papers, baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates,
    │                            dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, work_packages,
    │                            low_bar_review, human_gate, topic
    │                    writes: final_recommendation
    ▼
   END
```

---

### 4. Node Details: What Each Node Reads/Writes

| # | Node | Reads from state | Writes to state | Has LLM? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **intake** | `topic`, `trace_events` | `case_id`, `provider_profile`, `trace_events`, `errors` | No |
| 2 | **topic_parser** | `topic` | `topic_atoms`, `trace_events`, `errors`, `provider_profile` | Yes (`fast_json`) |
| 3 | **search_planner** | `topic`, `topic_atoms`, `search_plan` (for idempotency) | `search_plan`, `trace_events`, `errors`, `provider_profile` | Yes (`fast_json`) |
| 4 | **paper_retriever** (search_agent) | `search_plan` | `raw_results`, `paper_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `search_steps`, `trace_events` | Yes (React loop, ≤8 steps) |
| 5 | **quality_filter** | `paper_candidates` | `paper_candidates` (filtered), `filter_results`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic regex fallback) |
| 6 | **verify** | `topic`, `topic_atoms`, `paper_candidates` | `verified_papers`, `weak_papers`, `trace_events` | Yes (batched, `fast_json`) |
| 7 | **quality_gate** | `verified_papers`, `weak_papers`, `evidence_audit`, `citation_expansion_done` | `evidence_audit` (mirrors routing logic for traceability) | No (rule-based) |
| 8 | **targeted_repair** | `baseline_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `paper_candidates`, `verified_papers`, `evidence_audit`, `errors`, `search_plan` | `search_plan` (replacement), `evidence_audit` (repair_rounds+1), `trace_events` | Yes (`fast_json`) |
| 9 | **citation_expander** | `verified_papers`, `topic_atoms` | `seed_papers`, `expanded_papers`, `surveys_found`, `repos_found`, `citation_expansion_done`, `paper_candidates`, `trace_events` | No (Semantic Scholar API) |
| 10 | **dataset_repo_extractor** | `verified_papers` | `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `trace_events`, `evidence_audit` | Yes (per-paper, ≤8 papers) |
| 11 | **evidence_graph_builder** | `verified_papers`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_papers`, `surveys`, `work_packages` | `evidence_graph`, `evidence_audit`, `trace_events` | No (pure function) |
| 12 | **baseline_classifier** | `verified_papers` | `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_papers`, `surveys`, `evidence_audit`, `trace_events` | Yes (LLM reclassification on all-same-role edge case) |
| 13 | **feasibility_assessor** | `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `topic` | `feasibility_report`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 14 | **work_package** | `topic`, `topic_atoms`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `user_constraints` | `work_packages`, `evidence_audit`, `trace_events`, `errors` | Yes (`fast_json`) |
| 15 | **innovation_extractor** | `topic`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates` | `innovation_points`, `stitching_plan`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 16 | **sota_matcher** | `topic`, `baseline_candidates` | `sota_comparison`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 17 | **narrative_builder** | `topic`, `innovation_points`, `feasibility_report` | `research_narrative`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 18 | **low_bar_review** | `work_packages`, `verified_papers`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `evidence_graph` | `low_bar_review`, `work_packages` (filtered), `trace_events` | No (rule-based, drops hallucinated citations) |
| 19 | **optimization_advisor** | `topic`, `feasibility_report`, `innovation_points`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates` | `optimization_directions`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 20 | **devils_advocate** | `baseline_candidates`, `feasibility_report` | `review_report`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 21 | **human_gate** | `verified_papers`, `work_packages` | `human_gate`, `trace_events` | No (pass-through unless `HUMAN_GATE_ENABLED=true`) |
| 22 | **final_recommendation** | `topic`, `verified_papers`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `work_packages`, `low_bar_review`, `human_gate` | `final_recommendation`, `trace_events` | No (summary composition) |

---

### 5. Conditional Edges & Routing Logic

#### 5a. `_route_after_quality_gate` (from `quality_gate`)

**File:** `research_graph.py`, lines ~137–177

**Decision matrix — Round 1 (`citation_expansion_done=False`):**

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `n_papers == 0 AND n_total >= 3 AND repair_rounds < max_repair` (Fix 5) | `"repair"` → targeted_repair |
| `n_papers < 1 AND repair_rounds < max_repair` | `"repair"` → targeted_repair |
| `n_papers < 1 AND repair_rounds >= max_repair` | `"blocked"` → final_recommendation |
| `n_papers < 3 AND repair_rounds < max_repair` (sufficiency gate, Re2.4) | `"repair"` → targeted_repair |
| `n_papers >= 1` (have papers, do expansion before continuing) | `"citation_expander"` |

**Decision matrix — Round 2 (`citation_expansion_done=True`):**

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `n_papers < 1` | `"blocked"` → final_recommendation |
| `n_papers >= 1` | `"continue"` → dataset_repo_extractor |

`max_repair` is read from env var `PAPERAGENT_MAX_REPAIR_ROUNDS` (default 2).

#### 5b. `_route_after_review` (from `low_bar_review`)

**File:** `research_graph.py`, lines ~179–205

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `low_bar_review.status == "pass"` | `"ready"` → optimization_advisor |
| `repair_rounds >= max_repair` | `"blocked"` → final_recommendation |
| `total_evidence < 4` (baseline + dataset + repo + work_package counts) | `"repair"` → targeted_repair |
| `total_evidence >= 4` | `"ready"` → optimization_advisor |

#### 5c. `_route_after_feasibility` (from `feasibility_assessor`)

**File:** `research_graph.py`, lines ~207–217

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `feasibility_report.verdict == "not_recommended"` | `"optimization_advisor"` (skips work_package chain) |
| `feasibility_report.verdict == "feasible"` or `"risky"` | `"work_package"` |

#### 5d. `_route_after_devils` (from `devils_advocate`)

**File:** `research_graph.py`, lines ~222–255

Constants: `MAX_NARRATIVE_REVISIONS = 2`, `MAX_BLOCK_RETRIES = 1`

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `overall_verdict == "ACCEPT"` | `"human_gate"` |
| `narrative_revision_count >= 2` | `"human_gate"` |
| `feasibility_verdict == "not_recommended" AND verdict == "BLOCK"` | `"human_gate"` |
| `verdict == "MINOR_REVISION" AND revisions < 2` | `"narrative_builder"` (revision loop) |
| `verdict == "BLOCK" AND block_count <= 1` | `"optimization_advisor"` (optimization loop) |
| `verdict == "BLOCK" AND block_count > 1` | `"human_gate"` |
| Fallthrough | `"human_gate"` |

---

### 6. Loop/Retry Patterns

There are **three** distinct loop/retry patterns:

#### Loop 1: Repair Loop (targeted_repair → paper_retriever → quality_filter → verify → quality_gate)
```
quality_gate ──"repair"──► targeted_repair ──► paper_retriever ──► quality_filter ──► verify ──► quality_gate
```
- **Trigger:** quality_gate or low_bar_review determines insufficient papers/evidence
- **Termination:** `repair_rounds >= PAPERAGENT_MAX_REPAIR_ROUNDS` (default 2)
- **Counter field:** `evidence_audit.repair_rounds` (incremented by targeted_repair)

#### Loop 2: Citation Expansion (citation_expander → verify → quality_gate)
```
quality_gate ──"citation_expander"──► citation_expander ──► verify ──► quality_gate
```
- **Trigger:** first round of quality_gate has `n_papers >= 1` and `citation_expansion_done=False`
- **Termination:** One-shot — `citation_expansion_done` is set to `True` by citation_expander; second quality_gate call routes to "continue" or "blocked"
- **Guard field:** `citation_expansion_done`

#### Loop 3a: Narrative Revision Loop (devils_advocate → narrative_builder → low_bar_review → optimization_advisor → devils_advocate)
```
devils_advocate ──"narrative_builder"──► narrative_builder ──► low_bar_review ──► optimization_advisor ──► devils_advocate
```
- **Trigger:** devils_advocate returns `MINOR_REVISION` verdict
- **Termination:** `narrative_revision_count >= MAX_NARRATIVE_REVISIONS` (2)
- **Counter field:** `narrative_revision_count`

#### Loop 3b: BLOCK Optimization Loop (devils_advocate → optimization_advisor → devils_advocate)
```
devils_advocate ──"optimization_advisor"──► optimization_advisor ──► devils_advocate
```
- **Trigger:** devils_advocate returns `BLOCK` verdict
- **Termination:** `devils_advocate_block_count > MAX_BLOCK_RETRIES` (1) or `feasibility_report.verdict == "not_recommended"`
- **Counter field:** `devils_advocate_block_count`

#### Abort Paths (→ `final_recommendation` directly):
Both `quality_gate` ("blocked") and `low_bar_review` ("blocked") can route directly to `final_recommendation`, bypassing the entire analysis chain, when repair rounds are exhausted without sufficient evidence.

#### Parallel Fan-Out:
`work_package` fans out to **both** `innovation_extractor` and `sota_matcher`, then both fan in to `narrative_builder` (there are no explicit join/barrier nodes — LangGraph handles convergence implicitly by executing both parallel paths before `narrative_builder` which receives merged state).

---

### 7. Summary Statistics

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total registered nodes | 22 |
| Linear edges (static) | 16 |
| Conditional edges (branches) | 4 gateways |
| Conditional routing functions | 4 |
| Loop/retry patterns | 3 explicit + 1 (citation expander is one-shot) |
| Entry point | `START → intake` |
| Terminal | `final_recommendation → END` |
| Max repair rounds | 2 (configurable via `PAPERAGENT_MAX_REPAIR_ROUNDS`) |
| Max narrative revisions | 2 |
| Max BLOCK retries | 1 |
| Parallel execution paths | 1 (work_package → innovation_extractor + sota_matcher) |

# CODELY.md — PaperAgent Project Context

## Project Overview

**PaperAgent** (TopicPilot-CN) is an interactive evidence workbench for Chinese graduate students' thesis topic selection. Users input a single topic + target tier, and the system produces keyword decomposition, multi-source retrieval, evidence verification, citation expansion, feasibility assessment, and a traceable opening-proposal Markdown report.

**Not** a full thesis generator — it organizes papers, datasets, GitHub baselines, PDF/screenshot/web materials into an auditable evidence chain. All AI-extracted results default to `pending` and require human review before being promoted to `accepted/core/background`.

### Core Pipeline

```
Input topic → Keyword decomposition → Multi-source retrieval → Workbench review
            → URL verified → Evidence cards → Trace persistence
            → FinalPackage Markdown → ReportQuality 8-dim review
```

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Python ≥3.12 |
| Backend | FastAPI + uvicorn |
| Graph Engine | LangGraph (state machine for research pipeline) |
| Schema | Pydantic v2 |
| HTTP | httpx (async) |
| Frontend | Vanilla JS (mounted as StaticFiles) |
| Testing | pytest + pytest-asyncio + Playwright |
| Linting | ruff |
| Package Mgmt | uv (uv.lock) + setuptools |
| PDF Parsing | pypdf |

## Building and Running

### Environment Setup

```bash
python -m venv .venv
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pip install -e ".[dev]"
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m playwright install chromium
```

Copy `.env.example` → `.env` and fill in API keys (DeepSeek recommended as primary LLM).

### Start Backend + Frontend (one command)

```bash
# Windows
start_frontend.bat
# Or manually:
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m uvicorn app.main:app --app-dir apps/api --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18181
```

- Frontend: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/web/>
- API docs: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/docs>
- Health: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/health>

### Tests

```bash
# All tests
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest

# Backend only
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/api/tests -v

# Frontend e2e only
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/web/e2e -v

# Specific session
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/api/tests/test_session66_agent.py -v
```

Pytest config: `asyncio_mode=auto`, testpaths = `apps/api/tests`, `apps/web/e2e`. Markers: `react-web`, `legacy-web`, `re02`, `re03`, `network`.

### Linting

```bash
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m ruff check .
```

## Architecture

### Directory Structure

```
apps/
  api/
    app/
      main.py                     # FastAPI entry, CORS, route registration, static mount
      api/v1/research.py          # Main REST router (submit, status, SSE stream, evidence graph, papers)
      services/
        llm_router.py             # Provider profile → concrete provider mapping (fast_json / execution / premium_review)
        llm.py                    # Low-level LLM client (DeepSeek, StepFun, VOAPI)
        json_repair.py             # 3-phase JSON parsing: direct → reasoning scan → fallback formatter
        agents/
          graph/
            state.py               # ResearchState TypedDict — shared state for all graph nodes
            research_graph.py     # LangGraph builder (nodes + edges + conditional routing)
            nodes/                # 20 graph nodes (intake → topic_parser → search_planner → retrieve → quality_filter → verify → citation_expander → feasibility → ... → review)
          *.py                     # Agent modules (candidate_pool, evidence_review, retrieval_orchestrator, etc.)
          prompts/                 # LLM prompt templates
        retrieval/
          adapters/                # 7 search adapters: arxiv, crossref, github, openalex, semantic_scholar, huggingface, core
    tests/                         # 90+ test files, one per session/feature
  web/
    index.html                     # Vanilla JS frontend (single-page evidence workbench)
    e2e/                           # Playwright e2e tests
docs/                              # Demo scripts, deployment runbook, testing matrix, project scope
Plan/                              # SOP documents (Re3.0/Re3.1 design specs)
scripts/                           # Utility scripts (eval dataset builder, retrieval smoke test)
```

### LangGraph Pipeline

The research pipeline is a LangGraph state machine. Each node receives the shared `ResearchState` TypedDict and returns a partial patch. Nodes **must not** mutate state in place.

Key nodes (in `apps/api/app/services/agents/graph/nodes/`):

| Node | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `intake` | Receive topic + user constraints |
| `topic_parser` | LLM-based keyword decomposition (method/task/object words) |
| `search_planner` | Generate query matrix for multi-source search |
| `retrieve` | Fan-out to 7 adapters in parallel (arXiv, Crossref, GitHub, OpenAlex, S2, HuggingFace, Core) |
| `quality_filter` | Pre-filter raw results (heuristic + LLM judge) |
| `verify` | Multi-round paper verification (accept / weak_reject / reject) |
| `citation_expander` | Seed paper selection → citation/reference expansion |
| `dataset_repo_extractor` | Extract dataset/repo candidates from verified papers |
| `feasibility_assessor` | 5-tier feasibility judgment |
| `innovation_extractor` | Identify innovation points |
| `sota_matcher` | Compare against SOTA |
| `narrative_builder` | Construct research narrative |
| `optimization_advisor` | Suggest optimization directions |
| `devils_advocate_node` | Reflection loop (counter-based revision) |
| `review` | Final quality review |

### LLM Provider Routing

Profiles map to providers via `llm_router.py`:

| Profile | Default Provider | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `fast_json` | DeepSeek (fallback: StepFun) | JSON-producing nodes (parser, planner, verifier) |
| `execution` | StepFun | Simple execution, no final judgment |
| `premium_review` | VOAPI | Final sampling review only |

Controlled via env: `LLM_PROFILE`, `FAST_JSON_PRIMARY`, `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`, `STEPFUN_API_KEY`.

### API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| POST | `/api/v1/research/` | Submit a topic for background research graph run |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/` | List all case IDs with results on disk |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/status` | Check run status |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/state` | Full final ResearchState JSON |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/trace` | Per-node trace events |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/evidence-graph` | Evidence graph (nodes + edges) |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/stream` | SSE stream of node progress (real-time) |
| POST | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/papers` | Upload a user-known paper (enriched via Crossref/arXiv) |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/feasibility` | Feasibility report |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/review` | Final review report |

Results are persisted under `tmp_re13_eval/{case_id}/` (state.json, trace.json, evidence_graph.json).

## Development Conventions

### Hardcoding Bans (from `rules.md`)

- **No hardcoded domain fallbacks** — never default to `"deep learning"` or fixed domain strings.
- **No hardcoded `domain_map`** — LLM is the primary path, not pattern matching.
- **No short-keyword filtering** by length — "YOLO" (4), "SLAM" (4), "GAN" (3) must pass.
- **No domain-specific words in prompt examples** — LLM will mimic example direction.
- **No hardcoded regex/blacklist** for self-checking — use LLM judgment.

### Search Chain Rules

- All adapters share the same query list from `search_planner`.
- Empty queries → use topic text, never a fixed string.
- One adapter 429/timeout must not block the pipeline (other adapters proceed independently).
- GitHub results go to `repo_candidates`, not `verified_papers`.

### JSON Parsing Robustness (from `CLAUDE.md`)

- System prompts for reasoner models must be <100 tokens.
- `call_json` calls must pass `expected="dict"` for structured nodes.
- Prompt must end with an OUTPUT CONTRACT clause.
- 3-phase parse: direct → reasoning field scan → fallback formatter (with schema hint).
- No silent error swallowing — retries must log warnings.

### Async & Concurrency

- I/O-intensive operations must use `asyncio.gather` / `asyncio.Semaphore`.
- Cross-source searches (Crossref/GitHub/arXiv) are inherently parallel — no serial waiting.
- API calls need rate limiting (Semaphore 3-5 concurrent, exponential backoff on 429).
- Tasks >60s should be delegated to subagents; main thread does productive work while waiting.

### Testing Strategy (from `AGENTS.md`)

- Independent test cases with total runtime >60s **must** be parallelized via subagents.
- Single tests <10s or total <60s → run serially (subagent overhead not worth it).
- >10 test cases: first 3 get full assertions, rest degrade to smoke test (no crash + non-empty output).
- Main thread must not idle while subagents run — review code, write prompts, check docs.
- Results are batch-summarized, not processed one-by-one.

### API Compatibility

- New backend/provider/adapter must not modify or delete existing call code.
- New features coexist with old ones, switched via config/parameters.
- All changes must be backward-compatible.

### Compliance Boundaries

- `rejected` evidence is never cited; `pending` doesn't directly support; `failed verification` doesn't support.
- No bypassing paywalled databases (Semantic Scholar: metadata only; Kaggle: listing only).
- No uploading user files to third-party services.
- All LLM credentials read from `.env` (never committed to git).
- LLM path can degrade to heuristic fallback without crashing the service.

### Code Style

- Python 3.12+ features are expected (e.g., `str | None`, `dict[str, Any]`).
- `from __future__ import annotations` is used in most modules.
- Pydantic v2 for all schemas.
- Functions return partial state patches (LangGraph merge pattern), not in-place mutation.
- Extensive trace event logging (`trace_events` list in ResearchState).
--- End of Context from: CODELY.md ---

## Project Overview

**PaperAgent** (TopicPilot-CN) is an interactive evidence workbench for Chinese graduate students' thesis topic selection. Users input a single topic + target tier, and the system produces keyword decomposition, multi-source retrieval, evidence verification, citation expansion, feasibility assessment, and a traceable opening-proposal Markdown report.

**Not** a full thesis generator — it organizes papers, datasets, GitHub baselines, PDF/screenshot/web materials into an auditable evidence chain. All AI-extracted results default to `pending` and require human review before being promoted to `accepted/core/background`.

### Core Pipeline

```
Input topic → Keyword decomposition → Multi-source retrieval → Workbench review
            → URL verified → Evidence cards → Trace persistence
            → FinalPackage Markdown → ReportQuality 8-dim review
```

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Python ≥3.12 |
| Backend | FastAPI + uvicorn |
| Graph Engine | LangGraph (state machine for research pipeline) |
| Schema | Pydantic v2 |
| HTTP | httpx (async) |
| Frontend | Vanilla JS (mounted as StaticFiles) |
| Testing | pytest + pytest-asyncio + Playwright |
| Linting | ruff |
| Package Mgmt | uv (uv.lock) + setuptools |
| PDF Parsing | pypdf |

## Building and Running

### Environment Setup

```bash
python -m venv .venv
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pip install -e ".[dev]"
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m playwright install chromium
```

Copy `.env.example` → `.env` and fill in API keys (DeepSeek recommended as primary LLM).

### Start Backend + Frontend (one command)

```bash
# Windows
start_frontend.bat
# Or manually:
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m uvicorn app.main:app --app-dir apps/api --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18181
```

- Frontend: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/web/>
- API docs: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/docs>
- Health: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/health>

### Tests

```bash
# All tests
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest

# Backend only
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/api/tests -v

# Frontend e2e only
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/web/e2e -v

# Specific session
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/api/tests/test_session66_agent.py -v
```

Pytest config: `asyncio_mode=auto`, testpaths = `apps/api/tests`, `apps/web/e2e`. Markers: `react-web`, `legacy-web`, `re02`, `re03`, `network`.

### Linting

```bash
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m ruff check .
```

## Architecture

### Directory Structure

```
apps/
  api/
    app/
      main.py                     # FastAPI entry, CORS, route registration, static mount
      api/v1/research.py          # Main REST router (submit, status, SSE stream, evidence graph, papers)
      services/
        llm_router.py             # Provider profile → concrete provider mapping (fast_json / execution / premium_review)
        llm.py                    # Low-level LLM client (DeepSeek, StepFun, VOAPI)
        json_repair.py             # 3-phase JSON parsing: direct → reasoning scan → fallback formatter
        agents/
          graph/
            state.py               # ResearchState TypedDict — shared state for all graph nodes
            research_graph.py     # LangGraph builder (nodes + edges + conditional routing)
            nodes/                # 20 graph nodes (intake → topic_parser → search_planner → retrieve → quality_filter → verify → citation_expander → feasibility → ... → review)
          *.py                     # Agent modules (candidate_pool, evidence_review, retrieval_orchestrator, etc.)
          prompts/                 # LLM prompt templates
        retrieval/
          adapters/                # 7 search adapters: arxiv, crossref, github, openalex, semantic_scholar, huggingface, core
    tests/                         # 90+ test files, one per session/feature
  web/
    index.html                     # Vanilla JS frontend (single-page evidence workbench)
    e2e/                           # Playwright e2e tests
docs/                              # Demo scripts, deployment runbook, testing matrix, project scope
Plan/                              # SOP documents (Re3.0/Re3.1 design specs)
scripts/                           # Utility scripts (eval dataset builder, retrieval smoke test)
```

### LangGraph Pipeline

The research pipeline is a LangGraph state machine. Each node receives the shared `ResearchState` TypedDict and returns a partial patch. Nodes **must not** mutate state in place.

Key nodes (in `apps/api/app/services/agents/graph/nodes/`):

| Node | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `intake` | Receive topic + user constraints |
| `topic_parser` | LLM-based keyword decomposition (method/task/object words) |
| `search_planner` | Generate query matrix for multi-source search |
| `retrieve` | Fan-out to 7 adapters in parallel (arXiv, Crossref, GitHub, OpenAlex, S2, HuggingFace, Core) |
| `quality_filter` | Pre-filter raw results (heuristic + LLM judge) |
| `verify` | Multi-round paper verification (accept / weak_reject / reject) |
| `citation_expander` | Seed paper selection → citation/reference expansion |
| `dataset_repo_extractor` | Extract dataset/repo candidates from verified papers |
| `feasibility_assessor` | 5-tier feasibility judgment |
| `innovation_extractor` | Identify innovation points |
| `sota_matcher` | Compare against SOTA |
| `narrative_builder` | Construct research narrative |
| `optimization_advisor` | Suggest optimization directions |
| `devils_advocate_node` | Reflection loop (counter-based revision) |
| `review` | Final quality review |

### LLM Provider Routing

Profiles map to providers via `llm_router.py`:

| Profile | Default Provider | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `fast_json` | DeepSeek (fallback: StepFun) | JSON-producing nodes (parser, planner, verifier) |
| `execution` | StepFun | Simple execution, no final judgment |
| `premium_review` | VOAPI | Final sampling review only |

Controlled via env: `LLM_PROFILE`, `FAST_JSON_PRIMARY`, `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`, `STEPFUN_API_KEY`.

### API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| POST | `/api/v1/research/` | Submit a topic for background research graph run |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/` | List all case IDs with results on disk |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/status` | Check run status |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/state` | Full final ResearchState JSON |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/trace` | Per-node trace events |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/evidence-graph` | Evidence graph (nodes + edges) |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/stream` | SSE stream of node progress (real-time) |
| POST | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/papers` | Upload a user-known paper (enriched via Crossref/arXiv) |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/feasibility` | Feasibility report |
| GET | `/api/v1/research/{case_id}/review` | Final review report |

Results are persisted under `tmp_re13_eval/{case_id}/` (state.json, trace.json, evidence_graph.json).

## Development Conventions

### Hardcoding Bans (from `rules.md`)

- **No hardcoded domain fallbacks** — never default to `"deep learning"` or fixed domain strings.
- **No hardcoded `domain_map`** — LLM is the primary path, not pattern matching.
- **No short-keyword filtering** by length — "YOLO" (4), "SLAM" (4), "GAN" (3) must pass.
- **No domain-specific words in prompt examples** — LLM will mimic example direction.
- **No hardcoded regex/blacklist** for self-checking — use LLM judgment.

### Search Chain Rules

- All adapters share the same query list from `search_planner`.
- Empty queries → use topic text, never a fixed string.
- One adapter 429/timeout must not block the pipeline (other adapters proceed independently).
- GitHub results go to `repo_candidates`, not `verified_papers`.

### JSON Parsing Robustness (from `CLAUDE.md`)

- System prompts for reasoner models must be <100 tokens.
- `call_json` calls must pass `expected="dict"` for structured nodes.
- Prompt must end with an OUTPUT CONTRACT clause.
- 3-phase parse: direct → reasoning field scan → fallback formatter (with schema hint).
- No silent error swallowing — retries must log warnings.

### Async & Concurrency

- I/O-intensive operations must use `asyncio.gather` / `asyncio.Semaphore`.
- Cross-source searches (Crossref/GitHub/arXiv) are inherently parallel — no serial waiting.
- API calls need rate limiting (Semaphore 3-5 concurrent, exponential backoff on 429).
- Tasks >60s should be delegated to subagents; main thread does productive work while waiting.

### Testing Strategy (from `AGENTS.md`)

- Independent test cases with total runtime >60s **must** be parallelized via subagents.
- Single tests <10s or total <60s → run serially (subagent overhead not worth it).
- >10 test cases: first 3 get full assertions, rest degrade to smoke test (no crash + non-empty output).
- Main thread must not idle while subagents run — review code, write prompts, check docs.
- Results are batch-summarized, not processed one-by-one.

### API Compatibility

- New backend/provider/adapter must not modify or delete existing call code.
- New features coexist with old ones, switched via config/parameters.
- All changes must be backward-compatible.

### Compliance Boundaries

- `rejected` evidence is never cited; `pending` doesn't directly support; `failed verification` doesn't support.
- No bypassing paywalled databases (Semantic Scholar: metadata only; Kaggle: listing only).
- No uploading user files to third-party services.
- All LLM credentials read from `.env` (never committed to git).
- LLM path can degrade to heuristic fallback without crashing the service.

### Code Style

- Python 3.12+ features are expected (e.g., `str | None`, `dict[str, Any]`).
- `from __future__ import annotations` is used in most modules.
- Pydantic v2 for all schemas.
- Functions return partial state patches (LangGraph merge pattern), not in-place mutation.
- Extensive trace event logging (`trace_events` list in ResearchState).

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).

| `optimization_advisor` | `optimization_advisor_node` | `optimization_advisor.py` |
| `devils_advocate` | `devils_advocate_node` | `devils_advocate_node.py` |
| `human_gate` | `human_gate_node` | `content.py` |
| `final_recommendation` | `final_recommendation_node` | `content.py` |

---

### 3. Full Execution Graph (Entry to Exit)

**Entry point:** `START → intake`

**Terminal node:** `final_recommendation → END`

```
START
  │
  ▼

  │
  ▼
[topic_parser]                        reads: topic | writes: topic_atoms, trace_events

  ▼
[search_planner]                      reads: topic, topic_atoms | writes: search_plan, trace_events

  ▼
[paper_retriever / search_agent]      reads: search_plan | writes: raw_results, paper_candidates, repo_candidates, search_steps
  │
  ▼
[quality_filter]                      reads: paper_candidates | writes: paper_candidates (filtered), filter_results

  ▼
[verify / paper_verifier]             reads: topic, topic_atoms, paper_candidates | writes: verified_papers, weak_papers
  │
  ▼
[quality_gate]                        reads: verified_papers, weak_papers, evidence_audit, citation_expansion_done
  │                                    writes: evidence_audit
  ├── "repair" ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  ├── "citation_expander" ────────────────────────────────────────────┐                               │
  ├── "continue" ──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │                               │
  ├── "blocked" ──────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │  │                               │
  └── "END" ─────────────────── (terminal)                    │    │  │                               │
                                                               │    │  │                               │
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │  │                               │
    ▼                                                               │  │                               │
[final_recommendation]                                              │  │                               │
    │                                                               │  │                               │
    ▼                                                               │  │                               │
   END                                                              │  │                               │
                                                                    │  │                               │
    ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │                               │
    ▼                                                                  │                               │
[citation_expander]    reads: verified_papers, topic_atoms             │                               │
    │                   writes: seed_papers, expanded_papers,          │                               │
    │                           surveys_found, repos_found,            │                               │
    │                           citation_expansion_done, paper_candidates│                             │
    │                                                                  │                               │
    └──► [verify] ──► [quality_gate]  (second round with citation_expansion_done=True)              │
                                                                       │                               │
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘                               │
    ▼                                                                                                  │
[dataset_repo_extractor]  reads: verified_papers | writes: dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, evidence_audit
    │
    ▼
[evidence_graph_builder]  reads: verified_papers, dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, baseline_candidates, work_packages
    │                      writes: evidence_graph, evidence_audit
    ▼
[baseline_classifier]     reads: verified_papers | writes: baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates, dataset_papers, surveys, evidence_audit
    │
    ▼
[feasibility_assessor]    reads: baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates, dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, topic
    │                      writes: feasibility_report
    │
    ├── "work_package" ──────────────────────────────────────┐
    └── "optimization_advisor" ───────────────────────────┐  │
                                                          │  │
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
    ▼                                                        │
[work_package]  reads: topic, topic_atoms, baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates,
    │                   dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, user_constraints
    │            writes: work_packages, evidence_audit
    │
    ├──► [innovation_extractor]  reads: topic, baseline_candidates, parallel_candidates
    │          writes: innovation_points, stitching_plan
    │
    └──► [sota_matcher]  reads: topic, baseline_candidates
               writes: sota_comparison
    │
    ├── (fan-in) ────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    └── (fan-in) ────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
                                                        │   │
    ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
    ▼                                                       │
[narrative_builder]  reads: topic, innovation_points, feasibility_report
    │                 writes: research_narrative
    ▼
[low_bar_review]     reads: work_packages, verified_papers, baseline_candidates,
    │                        parallel_candidates, dataset_candidates, repo_candidates, evidence_graph
    │                 writes: low_bar_review, work_packages (filtered)
    │
    ├── "repair" ──► [targeted_repair] ──► [paper_retriever] (loops back to retrieval chain)
    ├── "ready" ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

                                                                               │

    ▼

    ▼

```

---

### 4. Node Details: What Each Node Reads/Writes

| # | Node | Reads from state | Writes to state | Has LLM? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **intake** | `topic`, `trace_events` | `case_id`, `provider_profile`, `trace_events`, `errors` | No |
| 2 | **topic_parser** | `topic` | `topic_atoms`, `trace_events`, `errors`, `provider_profile` | Yes (`fast_json`) |
| 3 | **search_planner** | `topic`, `topic_atoms`, `search_plan` (for idempotency) | `search_plan`, `trace_events`, `errors`, `provider_profile` | Yes (`fast_json`) |
| 4 | **paper_retriever** (search_agent) | `search_plan` | `raw_results`, `paper_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `search_steps`, `trace_events` | Yes (React loop, ≤8 steps) |
| 5 | **quality_filter** | `paper_candidates` | `paper_candidates` (filtered), `filter_results`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic regex fallback) |
| 6 | **verify** | `topic`, `topic_atoms`, `paper_candidates` | `verified_papers`, `weak_papers`, `trace_events` | Yes (batched, `fast_json`) |
| 7 | **quality_gate** | `verified_papers`, `weak_papers`, `evidence_audit`, `citation_expansion_done` | `evidence_audit` (mirrors routing logic for traceability) | No (rule-based) |
| 8 | **targeted_repair** | `baseline_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `paper_candidates`, `verified_papers`, `evidence_audit`, `errors`, `search_plan` | `search_plan` (replacement), `evidence_audit` (repair_rounds+1), `trace_events` | Yes (`fast_json`) |
| 9 | **citation_expander** | `verified_papers`, `topic_atoms` | `seed_papers`, `expanded_papers`, `surveys_found`, `repos_found`, `citation_expansion_done`, `paper_candidates`, `trace_events` | No (Semantic Scholar API) |
| 10 | **dataset_repo_extractor** | `verified_papers` | `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `trace_events`, `evidence_audit` | Yes (per-paper, ≤8 papers) |
| 11 | **evidence_graph_builder** | `verified_papers`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_papers`, `surveys`, `work_packages` | `evidence_graph`, `evidence_audit`, `trace_events` | No (pure function) |
| 12 | **baseline_classifier** | `verified_papers` | `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_papers`, `surveys`, `evidence_audit`, `trace_events` | Yes (LLM reclassification on all-same-role edge case) |
| 13 | **feasibility_assessor** | `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `topic` | `feasibility_report`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 14 | **work_package** | `topic`, `topic_atoms`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `user_constraints` | `work_packages`, `evidence_audit`, `trace_events`, `errors` | Yes (`fast_json`) |
| 15 | **innovation_extractor** | `topic`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates` | `innovation_points`, `stitching_plan`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 16 | **sota_matcher** | `topic`, `baseline_candidates` | `sota_comparison`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 17 | **narrative_builder** | `topic`, `innovation_points`, `feasibility_report` | `research_narrative`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 18 | **low_bar_review** | `work_packages`, `verified_papers`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `evidence_graph` | `low_bar_review`, `work_packages` (filtered), `trace_events` | No (rule-based, drops hallucinated citations) |
| 19 | **optimization_advisor** | `topic`, `feasibility_report`, `innovation_points`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates` | `optimization_directions`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 20 | **devils_advocate** | `baseline_candidates`, `feasibility_report` | `review_report`, `trace_events` | Yes (heuristic fallback) |
| 21 | **human_gate** | `verified_papers`, `work_packages` | `human_gate`, `trace_events` | No (pass-through unless `HUMAN_GATE_ENABLED=true`) |
| 22 | **final_recommendation** | `topic`, `verified_papers`, `baseline_candidates`, `parallel_candidates`, `dataset_candidates`, `repo_candidates`, `work_packages`, `low_bar_review`, `human_gate` | `final_recommendation`, `trace_events` | No (summary composition) |

---

### 5. Conditional Edges & Routing Logic

#### 5a. `_route_after_quality_gate` (from `quality_gate`)

**File:** `research_graph.py`, lines ~137–177

**Decision matrix — Round 1 (`citation_expansion_done=False`):**

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `n_papers == 0 AND n_total >= 3 AND repair_rounds < max_repair` (Fix 5) | `"repair"` → targeted_repair |
| `n_papers < 1 AND repair_rounds < max_repair` | `"repair"` → targeted_repair |
| `n_papers < 1 AND repair_rounds >= max_repair` | `"blocked"` → final_recommendation |
| `n_papers < 3 AND repair_rounds < max_repair` (sufficiency gate, Re2.4) | `"repair"` → targeted_repair |
| `n_papers >= 1` (have papers, do expansion before continuing) | `"citation_expander"` |

**Decision matrix — Round 2 (`citation_expansion_done=True`):**

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `n_papers < 1` | `"blocked"` → final_recommendation |
| `n_papers >= 1` | `"continue"` → dataset_repo_extractor |

`max_repair` is read from env var `PAPERAGENT_MAX_REPAIR_ROUNDS` (default 2).

#### 5b. `_route_after_review` (from `low_bar_review`)

**File:** `research_graph.py`, lines ~179–205

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `low_bar_review.status == "pass"` | `"ready"` → optimization_advisor |
| `repair_rounds >= max_repair` | `"blocked"` → final_recommendation |
| `total_evidence < 4` (baseline + dataset + repo + work_package counts) | `"repair"` → targeted_repair |
| `total_evidence >= 4` | `"ready"` → optimization_advisor |

#### 5c. `_route_after_feasibility` (from `feasibility_assessor`)

**File:** `research_graph.py`, lines ~207–217

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `feasibility_report.verdict == "not_recommended"` | `"optimization_advisor"` (skips work_package chain) |
| `feasibility_report.verdict == "feasible"` or `"risky"` | `"work_package"` |

#### 5d. `_route_after_devils` (from `devils_advocate`)

**File:** `research_graph.py`, lines ~222–255

Constants: `MAX_NARRATIVE_REVISIONS = 2`, `MAX_BLOCK_RETRIES = 1`

| Condition | Route |
|---|---|
| `overall_verdict == "ACCEPT"` | `"human_gate"` |
| `narrative_revision_count >= 2` | `"human_gate"` |
| `feasibility_verdict == "not_recommended" AND verdict == "BLOCK"` | `"human_gate"` |
| `verdict == "MINOR_REVISION" AND revisions < 2` | `"narrative_builder"` (revision loop) |
| `verdict == "BLOCK" AND block_count <= 1` | `"optimization_advisor"` (optimization loop) |
| `verdict == "BLOCK" AND block_count > 1` | `"human_gate"` |
| Fallthrough | `"human_gate"` |

---

### 6. Loop/Retry Patterns

There are **three** distinct loop/retry patterns:

#### Loop 1: Repair Loop (targeted_repair → paper_retriever → quality_filter → verify → quality_gate)
```
quality_gate ──"repair"──► targeted_repair ──► paper_retriever ──► quality_filter ──► verify ──► quality_gate
```
- **Trigger:** quality_gate or low_bar_review determines insufficient papers/evidence
- **Termination:** `repair_rounds >= PAPERAGENT_MAX_REPAIR_ROUNDS` (default 2)
- **Counter field:** `evidence_audit.repair_rounds` (incremented by targeted_repair)

#### Loop 2: Citation Expansion (citation_expander → verify → quality_gate)
```
quality_gate ──"citation_expander"──► citation_expander ──► verify ──► quality_gate
```
- **Trigger:** first round of quality_gate has `n_papers >= 1` and `citation_expansion_done=False`
- **Termination:** One-shot — `citation_expansion_done` is set to `True` by citation_expander; second quality_gate call routes to "continue" or "blocked"
- **Guard field:** `citation_expansion_done`

#### Loop 3a: Narrative Revision Loop (devils_advocate → narrative_builder → low_bar_review → optimization_advisor → devils_advocate)
```
devils_advocate ──"narrative_builder"──► narrative_builder ──► low_bar_review ──► optimization_advisor ──► devils_advocate
```
- **Trigger:** devils_advocate returns `MINOR_REVISION` verdict
- **Termination:** `narrative_revision_count >= MAX_NARRATIVE_REVISIONS` (2)
- **Counter field:** `narrative_revision_count`

#### Loop 3b: BLOCK Optimization Loop (devils_advocate → optimization_advisor → devils_advocate)
```
devils_advocate ──"optimization_advisor"──► optimization_advisor ──► devils_advocate
```
- **Trigger:** devils_advocate returns `BLOCK` verdict
- **Termination:** `devils_advocate_block_count > MAX_BLOCK_RETRIES` (1) or `feasibility_report.verdict == "not_recommended"`
- **Counter field:** `devils_advocate_block_count`

#### Abort Paths (→ `final_recommendation` directly):
Both `quality_gate` ("blocked") and `low_bar_review` ("blocked") can route directly to `final_recommendation`, bypassing the entire analysis chain, when repair rounds are exhausted without sufficient evidence.

#### Parallel Fan-Out:
`work_package` fans out to **both** `innovation_extractor` and `sota_matcher`, then both fan in to `narrative_builder` (there are no explicit join/barrier nodes — LangGraph handles convergence implicitly by executing both parallel paths before `narrative_builder` which receives merged state).

---

### 7. Summary Statistics

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total registered nodes | 22 |
| Linear edges (static) | 16 |
| Conditional edges (branches) | 4 gateways |
| Conditional routing functions | 4 |
| Loop/retry patterns | 3 explicit + 1 (citation expander is one-shot) |
| Entry point | `START → intake` |
| Terminal | `final_recommendation → END` |
| Max repair rounds | 2 (configurable via `PAPERAGENT_MAX_REPAIR_ROUNDS`) |
| Max narrative revisions | 2 |
| Max BLOCK retries | 1 |
| Parallel execution paths | 1 (work_package → innovation_extractor + sota_matcher) |

--- Context from: CODELY.md ---
# CODELY.md — PaperAgent Project Context

## Project Overview

**PaperAgent** (TopicPilot-CN) is an interactive evidence workbench for Chinese graduate students' thesis topic selection. Users input a single topic + target tier, and the system produces keyword decomposition, multi-source retrieval, evidence verification, citation expansion, feasibility assessment, and a traceable opening-proposal Markdown report.

**Not** a full thesis generator — it organizes papers, datasets, GitHub baselines, PDF/screenshot/web materials into an auditable evidence chain. All AI-extracted results default to `pending` and require human review before being promoted to `accepted/core/background`.

### Core Pipeline

```
Input topic → Keyword decomposition → Multi-source retrieval → Workbench review
            → URL verified → Evidence cards → Trace persistence
            → FinalPackage Markdown → ReportQuality 8-dim review
```

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Python ≥3.12 |
| Backend | FastAPI + uvicorn |
| Graph Engine | LangGraph (state machine for research pipeline) |
| Schema | Pydantic v2 |
| HTTP | httpx (async) |
| Frontend | Vanilla JS (mounted as StaticFiles) |
| Testing | pytest + pytest-asyncio + Playwright |
| Linting | ruff |
| Package Mgmt | uv (uv.lock) + setuptools |
| PDF Parsing | pypdf |

## Building and Running

### Environment Setup

```bash
python -m venv .venv
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pip install -e ".[dev]"
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m playwright install chromium
```

Copy `.env.example` → `.env` and fill in API keys (DeepSeek recommended as primary LLM).

### Start Backend + Frontend (one command)

```bash
# Windows
start_frontend.bat
# Or manually:
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m uvicorn app.main:app --app-dir apps/api --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18181
```

- Frontend: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/web/>
- API docs: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/docs>
- Health: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/health>

### Tests

```bash
# All tests
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest

# Backend only
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/api/tests -v

# Frontend e2e only
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/web/e2e -v

# Specific session
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/api/tests/test_session66_agent.py -v
```

Pytest config: `asyncio_mode=auto`, testpaths = `apps/api/tests`, `apps/web/e2e`. Markers: `react-web`, `legacy-web`, `re02`, `re03`, `network`.

### Linting

```bash
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m ruff check .
```

## Architecture

### Directory Structure

```
apps/
  api/
    app/
      main.py                     # FastAPI entry, CORS, route registration, static mount
      api/v1/research.py          # Main REST router (submit, status, SSE stream, evidence graph, papers)
      services/
        llm_router.py             # Provider profile → concrete provider mapping (fast_json / execution / premium_review)
        llm.py                    # Low-level LLM client (DeepSeek, StepFun, VOAPI)
        json_repair.py             # 3-phase JSON parsing: direct → reasoning scan → fallback formatter
        agents/
          graph/
            state.py               # ResearchState TypedDict — shared state for all graph nodes
            research_graph.py     # LangGraph builder (nodes + edges + conditional routing)
            nodes/                # 20 graph nodes (intake → topic_parser → search_planner → retrieve → quality_filter → verify → citation_expander → feasibility → ... → review)
          *.py                     # Agent modules (candidate_pool, evidence_review, retrieval_orchestrator, etc.)
          prompts/                 # LLM prompt templates
        retrieval/
          adapters/                # 7 search adapters: arxiv, crossref, github, openalex, semantic_scholar, huggingface, core
    tests/                         # 90+ test files, one per session/feature
  web/
```

# CODELY.md — PaperAgent Project Context

## Project Overview

**PaperAgent** (TopicPilot-CN) is an interactive evidence workbench for Chinese graduate students' thesis topic selection. Users input a single topic + target tier, and the system produces keyword decomposition, multi-source retrieval, evidence verification, citation expansion, feasibility assessment, and a traceable opening-proposal Markdown report.

**Not** a full thesis generator — it organizes papers, datasets, GitHub baselines, PDF/screenshot/web materials into an auditable evidence chain. All AI-extracted results default to `pending` and require human review before being promoted to `accepted/core/background`.

### Core Pipeline

```
Input topic → Keyword decomposition → Multi-source retrieval → Workbench review
            → URL verified → Evidence cards → Trace persistence
            → FinalPackage Markdown → ReportQuality 8-dim review
```

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Python ≥3.12 |
| Backend | FastAPI + uvicorn |
| Graph Engine | LangGraph (state machine for research pipeline) |
| Schema | Pydantic v2 |
| HTTP | httpx (async) |
| Frontend | Vanilla JS (mounted as StaticFiles) |
| Testing | pytest + pytest-asyncio + Playwright |
| Linting | ruff |
| Package Mgmt | uv (uv.lock) + setuptools |
| PDF Parsing | pypdf |

## Building and Running

### Environment Setup

```bash
python -m venv .venv
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pip install -e ".[dev]"
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m playwright install chromium
```

Copy `.env.example` → `.env` and fill in API keys (DeepSeek recommended as primary LLM).

### Start Backend + Frontend (one command)

```bash
# Windows
start_frontend.bat
# Or manually:
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m uvicorn app.main:app --app-dir apps/api --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18181
```

- Frontend: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/web/>
- API docs: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/docs>
- Health: <http://127.0.0.1:18181/health>

### Tests

```bash
# All tests
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest

# Backend only
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/api/tests -v

# Frontend e2e only
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/web/e2e -v

# Specific session
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest apps/api/tests/test_session66_agent.py -v
```

Pytest config: `asyncio_mode=auto`, testpaths = `apps/api/tests`, `apps/web/e2e`. Markers: `react-web`, `legacy-web`, `re02`, `re03`, `network`.

### Linting

```bash
.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m ruff check .
```

```
apps/
```
